Title: FY24/25 SM-3 Block IB AURs
Description: Missile Defense Agency – 12 March 2026 Contract Announcement Raytheon, Tucson, Arizona is being awarded a noncompetitive Fixed-Price-Incentive-Fee modification (HQ0851-24-C-0001), definitizing two previously announced undefinitized contract actions (P00008 and P00014) for Standard Missile (SM-3) Block IB production. The value of this contract modification is $266,912,456, increasing the previously announced value of $1,099,000,000 to a total value of $1,365,912,456. The total definitized value of this award increases the total existing contract value from $1,948,713,505 by $1,365,912,456 to $3,314,625,961. Under this modification, the contractor will procure and deliver an additional quantity of 23 SM-3 Block IB All-Up Rounds (AURs) for a total of 78 SM-3 Block IB AURs. This contract includes one-time costs to restart the SM-3 IB production line. The work will be performed in Tucson, Arizona and Huntsville, Alabama with an expected completion date of May 2030. Fiscal 2024 and 2025 Procurement Funds are being used to fully fund this effort upon award. The Missile Defense Agency, Huntsville, Alabama, is the contracting activity (# HQ0851-24-C-0001). Contracting office point of contact: Corey Lisenbee (983) 283-1014 Report Control Symbol: DD-LA-(AR) 1279 Public Release Number: 25-MDA-12276

Title: JOPES/TransViz
Awardee: TRILLION TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS INC Reston VA 20191 USA
Description: USTRANSCOM has awarded a firm fixed price contract, HTC71126CE167, to Trillion Technology Solutions Inc. The contract provides JOPES that formally established policies and procedures that guide conventional operations planning and execution and TransViz provides transportation requirements and plan analysis tool to work with JOPES for global force movements. The contract was competitively procured. The primary location of performance is Scott AFB, with an expected completion date of 30 September 2030, including the base period and four one-year option periods. Additionally, contract includes FAR 52.217-8, Option to Extend Services, for a maximum potential completion date of 31 March 2031. Total contract value is $5,659,048.50. The contracting activity is the U.S. Transportation Command, Directorate of Acquisition, Scott Air Force Base, Illinois.

Title: Fort Totten - Refuse and Recycling Services
PopStreetAddress: Ernie Pyle USARC/ AMSA#12 200 Duane Road, Fort Totten NY.11359
Awardee: MRT BWR, CORP. 7310 EDSALL AVE Glendale NY 11385 USA
Description: The Army Contracting Command ? NJ, Warfighter Support Division has a new requirement for Refuse and Recycling Services to be performed at Ernie Pyle USARC/ AMSA#12 200 Duane Road, Fort Totten NY.11359, in support of the 99th Readiness Division. Refuse and Recycling Services are provided to maintain the facility in a clean, neat, free of unwanted debris and trash and professional appearance in efforts to enhance public relations and attractiveness. Refuse and Recycling Services will also ensure a healthy environment for all on-site personnel minimizing illness and contamination from uncollected trash and any possible hazardous material in need of disposal. The expected period of performance is one 12-month base period and four 12-month option periods. The purpose of this update is to correct the post closing to reflect what was on the solicitation document, no amendment is required for this.

Repair Antennas for U.S. Coast Guard Aircraft HC-27J
RSNF InKSA Support Services
Awardee: SCIENCE APPLICATIONS INTERNATIONAL CORPORATION 12010 SUNSET HILLS RD FL 4 Reston VA 20190 USA
Description: This acquisition will support the Program Executive Office (PEO) for Command, Control, Communications, Computers, and Intelligence (C4I) International Integration Program Office (PMW 740) requirement to procure Program Management, Systems Engineering, Operations and Maintenance, and Logistics support services for its portfolio of Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA) Foreign Military Sales (FMS) C4I, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C4ISR) cases with an option for Communication Security (COMSEC) custodial services. PMW 740 uses FMS and other Security Cooperation processes to promote interoperability with coalition and friendly nations by procuring, integrating, testing, training, and supporting integrated C4ISR capabilities. This requirement includes In-KSA support services for current and future FMS customer requirements. The United States (U.S.) Government (USG) and KSA have negotiated international agreements and established FMS cases to upgrade and refurbish the Royal Saudi Naval Forces (RSNF) C4ISR Systems (herein RSNF Systems) and ancillary subsystems.

Title: C-130 JMATS IV
Awardee: LOCKHEED MARTIN CORPORATION Orlando FL 32825 USA
Description: Lockheed Martin Rotary and Mission Systems, Orlando, Florida, was awarded a$1,900,000,000 indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract for C-130J Maintenanceand Training System (JMATS) IV. This contract provides for the continuation of production,modernization, sustainment, and support, via the Training System Support Center of C-130JAircrew Training Devices, Maintenance Training Devices, and Maintenance and AircrewTraining System. Work will be performed at Little Rock Air Force Base, Arkansas; DyessAFB, Texas; Marine Corps Air Station Cherry Point, North Carolina; Marine Corps AirStation Kaneohe Bay, Hawaii; Coast Guard Air Station Elizabeth City, North Carolina;Quonset Point Air National Guard, Rhode Island; Keesler Air Force Base, Mississippi;Marine Corps Air Station Miramar, California; Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base FortWorth, Texas; Ramstein Air Base, Germany; Yokota Air Base, Japan; and Marine Corps AirStation Iwakuni, Japan, and is expected to be completed by Feb. 28, 2039. The JMATS IVcontract will be composed of one five-year base ordering period and one five-year optionperiod, with an additional three years for the completion of all deliveries. Fiscal 2026operations and maintenance funds in the amount of $1,000 per for the initial task order arebeing obligated at the time of award. The Air Force Lifecycle Management Center,Simulators Division, Wright-Patterson Air Force Base, Ohio, is the contracting activity(FA8621-26-D-B001). (Awarded Feb. 27, 2026)

Description: GENERAL: C.W. Bill Young Veterans Medical Center located at 10000 Bay Pines Blvd. Bay Pines, Fl. 33744, and Lee County Veterans Healthcare Center located at 2489 Diplomat Pkwy E, Cape Coral, FL 33909 has a requirement for an Updated Water Management Plan, Water Management Update, (Audit) and New Water Management Plan for both Bay Pines VHA and Lee County Healthcare Center. The Bay Pines and Lee County Healthcare center need to meet the ST108 standard and to have continued support after the water management plans are created. BACKGROUND: VHA directive 1061 requires all VHA facilities where patients, visitors and employees spend the night and for other services (i.e. Dental) to have a Healthcare Associated Legionella Disease (HCA LD) prevention plan (also known as a Water Management Plan (WMP).

Title: PA154/PA157/PA191 Metal Containers
Awardee: MOUNTAIN HORSE LLC Colorado Springs CO 80903 USA
Description: This effort is for the award of Manufacture and Delivery of PA154/PA157/PA191 Mortar Ammunition Metal Containers, which are gasket sealed rectangular metal containers that serve as the outer pack for 60mm/81mm/120mm Mortar Cartridges. The PA154 Metal Container supports the 120mm Smoke and Illumination (Illum) Mortar Cartridges, M929/M930/M983. The PA 157 Metal Container supports the 81mm Smoke and Illum. Mortar Cartridges M819/M853A1/M816. The PA191 Metal Container supports the 60mm Smoke and Illum. Mortar Cartridges M722A1/M721/M767. Additionally, the PA154 holds two 120mm Mortar Cartridges, the PA157 holds three 81mm Mortar Cartridges, and the PA191 holds eight 60mm Mortar Cartridges.

Title: Award Synopsis of the National Industrial Security Program Operating Manual (NISPOM) Compliance Upgrade Delivery Order under Raytheon Corporate BOA
Awardee: RAYTHEON COMPANY El Segundo CA 90245 USA
Description: The Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR) has awarded a Cost Plus Fixed Fee Delivery Order under Basic Ordering Agreement (BOA) N00019-20-G-0007 to The Raytheon Company. This task order is for the procurement of the tasks required to upgrade APG-79 radar test stations to be compliant with the latest National Industrial Security Program Operating Manuel (NISPOM) requirements. In order to remain NISPOM compliance, four test stations received upgrades to Windows 10. This contract was procured non-competitively pursuant to 10 U.S.C 2304(c)(1). Raytheon is the sole designer, developer, and integrator of the AN/APG-65/-65(V)/-73/-79(V)1/-79(V)4 Radar Systems. Raytheon, as the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) is the sole designer, developer, manufacturer, and supplier of the F/A-18 and EA-18G radar systems and is the only source with the knowledge, expertise, and on-site personnel necessary to accomplish this effort within the timeframe required. This synopsis is for informational purposes only.
